title: Aberdeen Group Engagement Letter — Maxtor Pools of Storage Executive Evangelism Program
author: Peter S. Kastner; Sandra Tubbs / Aberdeen Group
abstract: A formal engagement letter from Aberdeen Group to Maxtor Corporation's VP of Worldwide Corporate Marketing, Stephen DiFranco, outlining a $114K four-phase program to create and evangelize the 'Pools of Storage' / midline storage market category. The program encompassed a strategy workshop, interactive toolkit development, 100–250-respondent message-testing surveys, and a US/Europe press evangelization tour. Crucially, it articulated the SAS/SATA backplane as the enabling hardware architecture for a new enterprise storage tier.
importance_rationale: First documented go-to-market blueprint for creating the midline/tiered-storage market category; articulated SAS/SATA mixed-backplane architecture as a standard years before it became universal, and established Aberdeen as the intellectual anchor for an entire new storage tier.
relevance_rationale: The four-phase methodology — lexicon creation, toolkit, message testing, press evangelism — remains a template for analyst-led market-category creation. Specific pricing and tool details are dated, but the strategic playbook is instructive.
prescience_rationale: The SAS/SATA backplane vision proved exactly correct: mixed SAS/SATA backplanes became the universal enterprise server standard from 2004 onward. The lexicon ('Online, Midline, Nearline, Offline') entered industry vocabulary. The market category Maxtor and Aberdeen set out to create — tiered/midline storage — became a dominant enterprise storage architecture by 2006-2011.
